COURT ORDERS THAT DEFENDANT JEAN DENOYER AND HIS EMPLOYEES, AGENST, AND PERSONS ACTING WITH HIM OR IN HIS BEHALF ARE ENJOINED AND RESTRAINED FROM DISTRIBUTING OR TRANSFERRING FUNDS FROM LLC'S OPERATING ACCOUNT TO HIMSELF, JEAN DENOYER, TO ANY LLC MEMBER, OR TO ANY OTHER PERSON OR ENTITY AFFILIATED WITH THEM OR ACTING ON THEIR BEHALF, DURING THE PENDENCY OF THIS ACTION. THE COURT DENIES THE APPLICATION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT PLAINTIFF FAISAL AL-HEJAILAN SHALL FILE WITH THE CLERK OF COURT A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $60, 000.00 PENDING TRIAL OF THIS ACTION. HE SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM RECEIPT OF NOTICE OF THE ORDER TO DO SO, DURING WHICH TIME THE COURT'S PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION/TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER SHALL BE IN PLACE.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Deft'S & X-Comp'S To Compel Supplemental Answers To First Sets Of Written Discovery Req. To Plntf And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sung E. Shim for defendant and moving party: Jean Denoyer
Benjamin J. Riley for plaintiff and opposing party: Faisal Al-Hejailan and cross-defendant: Martin Thesing

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Defendant Jean Denoyer's motion to compel supplemental responses to form and special interrogatories, requests for admissions, and request for production of documents (set #1)-as to plaintiff Al-Hejailan is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to form interrogatories 12.1, 14.1, and 17.1 (Requests #10, 88, 90, 98-101, 105). Plaintiff is ordered to serve supplemental responses within 15 days of notice of this order. In addition, plaintiff is ordered to provide a privilege log as agreed to in the letter of March 9, 2006.

The motion is denied as to special interrogatories 63-79, 87, and requests for admissions 40-45. The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Deft & X-Comp'S To Compel Initial Answers To Written Discovery Requests And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sung E. Shim for defendant and moving party: Jean Denoyer
Benjamin J. Riley for plaintiff and opposing party: Faisal Al-Hejailan and cross-defendant: Martin Thesing

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Jean Denoyer's motion to compel initial responses to form and special interrogatories (set #1, request for production of documents (set #1), requests for admissions (set #1)-as to cross-defendant Thesing and special interrogatories (set #2)-as to plaintiff Al-Hejailan is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is moot as responses have been served to form and special interrogatories (set #1), request for production of documents (set #1), and requests for admissions (set #1). The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of said responses. The motion is granted as to special interrogatories (set #2). Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond in a timely fashion and to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1165 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
